qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Integer overflow in HT/J2K decompression offset and stride calculations leads to heap buffer overflow
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an integer overflow vulnerability in HT/J2K decompression (internal_ht.cpp), while the advisory describes a heap information disclosure vulnerability in PXR24 decompression (internal_pxr24.c). These are different compression formats, different vulnerable functions, different root causes (integer overflow vs unchecked decompressed size), and different code locations.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a heap information disclosure vulnerability in PXR24 decompression (internal_pxr24.c:undo_pxr24_impl) where the decompressed size returned by exr_uncompress_buffer() is ignored and the scratch buffer is read based on header-derived expected size. The htj2k_integer_overflow finding describes a different vulnerability (integer overflow in HT/J2K decompression in internal_ht.cpp). The finding does not reference the PXR24 compression path, the undo_pxr24_impl function, the missing decompressed size check, or the LIBDEFLATE_SHORT_OUTPUT handling issue.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-34543 describes vulnerability in internal_pxr24.c:undo_pxr24_impl function (lines 279-287) where outSize return value is ignored
Advisory root cause: PXR24 decompression reads uninitialized heap memory when exr_uncompress_buffer returns LIBDEFLATE_SHORT_OUTPUT
Advisory also identifies flaw in compression.c:202-205 where LIBDEFLATE_SHORT_OUTPUT is treated as success

htj2k_integer_overflow finding describes vulnerability in internal_ht.cpp (lines 186-189, 211, 215-216, 279-280) - different file, different compression format, different root cause (integer overflow vs unchecked size)
